headline: AI agents moving from hype to production; adoption challenges become the bottleneck

stories.title: AI Agents Shift From Tool to Autonomous Co-worker
stories.summary: Ethan Mollick says companies need to stop treating AI as a better chatbot and start managing it as a system that performs hours of autonomous work. AI agents are reshaping strategy, coding, and risk assessment across enterprises.
stories.takeaway: The real business value from AI comes from agents that work independently, not chatbots that answer questions. Companies that treat AI as an autonomous co-worker will pull ahead.

stories.title: The Last Mile Problem: Why AI Transformation Stalls
stories.summary: Most companies are stuck in pilots—the real bottleneck isn't model quality or data, it's actually changing how work gets done. Fundamental workflow redesign is key to unlocking EBIT impact from gen AI.
stories.takeaway: Having great AI tech means nothing if you don't restructure the business around it. The hardest part of AI adoption is organizational change, not technology.

stories.title: OpenAI's Competitive Gamble: Can It Survive Without Unique Tech?
stories.summary: Benedict Evans argues OpenAI faces a critical problem—no unique technology, narrow user engagement, and zero network effects while incumbents have matched the tech and product distribution. The real value will come from new experiences OpenAI can't invent alone.
stories.takeaway: OpenAI's dominance isn't guaranteed. Microsoft, Google, and Meta are catching up fast. The winner will be whoever invents the new experiences on top of AI models.

stories.title: MIT's AI Energy Crisis: Can Infrastructure Keep Up?
stories.summary: Data centers are becoming a grid asset, not just company infrastructure. IEA projects AI energy use could hit 945 TWh by 2030 (up from 415 TWh in 2024), forcing utilities to negotiate 100+ megawatt connections and sparking community pushback.
stories.takeaway: AI capex and energy constraints are real bottlenecks now. The companies that secure reliable power and computational resources will win the infrastructure race.

watchNext.title: Agentic Commerce: $3-5 Trillion Opportunity by 2030
watchNext.why: McKinsey's research shows AI-mediated discovery and evaluation are scaling fast in e-commerce. This is where the next wave of AI value capture happens—not in chat but in autonomous purchasing workflows.

watchNext.title: 2026 Is the Death of the Prompt Box
watchNext.why: a16z predicts the next wave of AI apps will have zero visible prompting—they'll observe your behavior and act proactively. This shift from instruction-based to intent-based AI is the interface reckoning everyone's waiting for.
